notes: 2026-07-03 live widget cleanup scanner reports zero matches across all categories; stored snapshot refreshed so future agents do not chase stale 2026-07-01 candidates.

2026-06-15 stored scanner snapshot reported zero matches across the then-tracked widget cleanup categories; do not treat that historical note as current without rerunning the live scanner.

2026-06-15 whole-project analyzer hygiene pass applied 65 dart fix changes across import ordering, redundant default arguments, and initializing formals; flutter analyze --no-fatal-infos now reports no issues.
Raw test timing calls now route through shared pumpFeatureUi/pumpFeatureUiFor helpers in manual QA, golden, and UI capture harnesses.

Definition catalog now reports zero consolidation candidates after local private-helper collision renames and centralizing duplicate date formats in AppTimeFormatters. See docs/audit_registry/consolidation_candidates.json.

pending.title: Reconcile production event trigger types before the next full Functions rollout
pending.summary: The 2026-07-22 production organizer rollout safely stopped two broad deploy attempts because live onEventInviteLinkWritten and onEventWaitlistOfferWritten are HTTPS functions while current source exports background Firestore triggers under the same names. Organizer-owned functions were deployed with an exact allowlist; the mismatched live functions were not replaced or deleted.
acceptance: inventory the live trigger type, region, runtime, and consumers for both function names
choose a no-downtime rename or explicitly approved replacement sequence rather than forcing an in-place type change
exercise invite-link and waitlist-offer behavior in staging before production
prove a full manifest-driven Functions deploy plan no longer stops on trigger-type drift
retain rollback evidence for any live function replacement

pending.title: Cut organizer intake claim targets over to canonical organizer documents
pending.summary: The public listing policy now fails closed unless a published listing carries an enabled claim capability backed by a canonical organizers/{id} promotion receipt. The organizer intake promotion and sync pipeline still emits clubs/{id} claim targets, while the canonical claim callable operates on organizers/{id}; those legacy receipts therefore cannot safely enable website claim submission.
acceptance: make organizer intake and promotion execution produce a canonical organizers/{id} claim target while retaining any released-client clubs/{id} projection only as explicit compatibility output
make the promotion receipt, generated website projection, and claim callable agree on the same organizers/{id} target
cover enabled canonical and disabled legacy/missing receipt cases in generator and public-surface behavior tests
prove a staging website claim succeeds through Firebase Auth and App Check against the canonical organizer target
keep every clubs/{id}-only receipt fail-closed for website claim capability

pending.title: Verify canonical organizer targets before enabling public reviews
pending.summary: The generated website projection now carries a review target capability that fails closed independently from claim, review-read, review-write, and runtime capability. Current production organizer-intake readiness reports the canonical targets as write-needed, so generated listings truthfully keep public review reads and writes disabled until a read-only receipt confirms an organizers/{id} or compatibility clubs/{id} document accepted by the callable.
acceptance: produce a current read-only readiness receipt that proves each published listing has an organizers/{id} or explicitly supported clubs/{id} review target
keep claim target readiness independent so a clubs/{id}-only target may enable reviews without enabling canonical organizer claims
make generated target, read, and write capabilities agree with the receipt and projection schema invariants
prove staging review list and moderated submit callables succeed for every enabled target kind
retain fail-closed runtime, static-route, and matrix coverage for missing, stale, or contradictory target evidence

pending.summary: Operational remainder split out of MATCH-CHAT-REMAINING-001 once its engineering scope was complete. These need a real device and live Firestore data, so they are the owner's to execute, not agent-closeable: (1) device QA on the live Chats list + thread; (2) inspect and clean seeded duplicate match/message data on the active phone account - per the original item's guard, do NOT treat synthetic duplicates as a UI bug until the Firestore documents and seed scripts (tool/demo/seed_demo_data.mjs, docs/demo_data_seeding.md) are inspected; (3) profile-thumbnail backfill over real data (the generator from DASHBOARD-RUN-HYPE-THUMBNAILS-001 exists; this is the one-time data migration). No code change is blocked on these - they are verification/ops only.

next_up.title: Restore Host Events to the canonical visual threshold
next_up.summary: The functional Host Events workspace remains complete, but the integrated sliver, option-group, and app-shell consolidation moved its deterministic reference result from 15.24% mismatch / 15.20 meanDelta to 14.74% mismatch / 18.63 meanDelta. The mismatch ratio improved while mean delta crossed the unchanged canonical limit of 18. An 18% / 19 regression ceiling keeps CI sensitive without falsely claiming canonical parity.

completed.title: Migrate reviewed manual Riverpod families to code generation

Provider names, family/query equality, lifecycle, .future access, and override contracts remain stable under a focused ProviderContainer test; generated outputs are checked in and the provider graph review no longer carries those planned decisions.

completed.title: Migrate Flutter product prose into governed copy catalogs
completed.summary: Closed with 2,811 typed ARB messages, marketing-owned native/notification/questionnaire/structured-domain JSON catalogs, generated Event Success and event-policy content, semantic build-time error rendering, and an empty mobile ownership baseline. The scanner is now a zero-debt gate rather than a ratchet; exact developer fixtures and canonical reference data remain the only file-level exemptions.

completed.title: Implement the Host v2 Events lifecycle workspace
completed.summary: Closed with a provider-free HostEventsWorkspaceState and lifecycle UI over the existing club event stream: New, exact-policy-gated non-persisted Repeat, minute-refreshed Upcoming/Live/Past filtering, exact boundary classification, cancelled exclusion, month/year groups, activity spines, live/today date blocks, bounded fill progress, filter-specific empty/error/loading states, and route-safe Manage navigation. Repeat clears the date, copies only form-representable policy shapes, and never assumes EventDraft identity. Verified by focused analyzer, focused Events/create tests, routed 390x812 capture, and the canonical strict comparison at 15.24% mismatch / 15.20 meanDelta with no regressionThresholds.

completed.title: Separate signed-in uid resolution from downstream loading states
completed.summary: Verified current event uid-gated surfaces no longer conflate auth loading, signed-out, and downstream event loading: CalendarScreen and SavedEventsScreen branch on uidProvider loading/error before reading event repositories, signed-out users get empty states without querying user event providers, and signed-in users retain separate downstream loading/error states. Verified: flutter analyze --no-fatal-infos lib/events/presentation/calendar/calendar_screen.dart lib/events/presentation/saved_events_screen.dart test/calendar/calendar_screen_test.dart test/events/calendar_screen_test.dart test/events/saved_events_screen_test.dart; flutter test --reporter compact test/calendar/calendar_screen_test.dart test/events/saved_events_screen_test.dart test/events/calendar_screen_test.dart.

completed.title: Separate auth loading from signed-out and empty event states
completed.summary: Verified CalendarScreen and SavedEventsScreen distinguish uidProvider auth loading, auth error, signed-out empty, signed-in empty, downstream event loading, and downstream event error. Existing regression tests cover auth-loading skeletons and auth-error copy before signed-out/empty copy for both screens. Verified: flutter analyze --no-fatal-infos lib/events/presentation/calendar/calendar_screen.dart lib/events/presentation/saved_events_screen.dart test/calendar/calendar_screen_test.dart test/events/calendar_screen_test.dart test/events/saved_events_screen_test.dart; flutter test --reporter compact test/calendar/calendar_screen_test.dart test/events/saved_events_screen_test.dart test/events/calendar_screen_test.dart.

completed.title: Close remaining new-widget Widgetbook/catalog inventory gaps
completed.summary: Closed the current HEAD^ -> working-tree new-widget inventory. The scanner now reports 37 added widget classes, 37 public, 0 private, 37 covered, 0 Widget-returning helpers, and 0 unresolved items. Added exact-name Widgetbook and docs/widget_catalog.md coverage for ExploreScreenEmptyState, ExploreClearAction, and ExploreEventsEmptySliver, regenerated widgetbook/lib/main.directories.g.dart, and refreshed docs/audit_registry/new_widget_inventory_scan.json. The earlier 274-unresolved backlog snapshot was stale after subsequent Widgetbook/catalog work.

completed.title: Razorpay webhook + reconciliation sweep + pending-order tracking
completed.summary: Closes the charged-but-unbooked gap when the client verification callback is missed. Added: razorpayWebhook (onRequest, HMAC-SHA256 raw-body verify; payment.captured -> shared fulfillment, payment.failed -> mark pending order failed), reconcileRazorpayOrders (15-min onSchedule sweep of stranded orders, bounded to 25), the razorpayPendingOrders tracking doc (schema + deny-all rules + status/createdAt composite index), and a shared fulfillRazorpayPayment helper invoked by callback/webhook/sweep. Adversarial review after merge found 2 issues, both fixed and regression-tested: HIGH - paidCount could double-increment under callback/webhook/sweep races (non-transactional counter bump fired before the status flip); the completed-write + paidCount increment now run inside a single paymentRef transaction so exactly one caller wins the non-terminal->completed transition and bumps the counter once. LOW - payment.failed moved orders out of 'pending', stranding a rare same-order recapture; the sweep now queries status in [pending, failed] and markRazorpayPendingOrder only advances permitted transitions (failed<-pending; expired<-pending|failed) so 'failed' stays sweep-eligible yet still settles to terminal 'expired'. Verified: functions build + lint clean, 357 tests green (incl. new fulfillment race + failed-then-recaptured regression tests). You deploy (Razorpay key_secret + webhook secret already in Firebase Secret Manager; ship the razorpayPendingOrders index).

completed.title: Unify top-level tab header and first-content spacing
completed.summary: Centralized the repeated tab-screen spacing drift (title/subtitle, pinned search/filter rows, control-to-first-content) into shared tokens - CatchInsets.screenTitleBlock/screenTitleBlockCompact/screenControlRow/screenControlRail/pageBodyHero/chatListTileVertical, CatchGaps.headerTitleToSubtitle, and CatchLayout.chatListAvatarExtent/chatListTextGap/chatListDividerInset - and migrated swipe_hub_screen, chat_list_tile, explore_filter_rail, and the chats sliver header onto them while keeping per-screen CatchSliverHeader height contracts for long-title composability. Folded-in 2026-05-12 scanner follow-ups also confirmed resolved: the location picker now uses the CatchField primitive (no raw TextField), and photo_slot already carries explicit Semantics(button:)/ExcludeSemantics on its tappable regions. Verified: flutter analyze clean. You deploy.

completed.title: Close remaining match and chat audit follow-ups (engineering scope)
completed.summary: Engineering scope discharged after grounding all six sub-tasks against current code. (a) matches-count badge -> presence-or-rename: RESOLVED - the nav badge already shows a real unread message count (totalUnreadCountProvider) and the host inbox chip shows real hostUnreadCount; there is no fake presence/matches-count badge, so the conservative-safe-default (never claim presence we don't have) is already met. (b) chat-tile width/separators/surfaces vs shared primitives: DONE - chat_list_tile is fully tokenized (SCREEN-SPACING-SYSTEM-001), and the new-matches rail's last raw values (avatar size 64, ring width 2) were routed to CatchLayout.chatNewMatchAvatarExtent + CatchStroke.underline, unifying its 'new' emphasis ring with the list tile. (c) new-match horizontal rail: IMPLEMENTED - chat_new_matches_rail.dart (CatchHorizontalRail) is built and wired to the chat route. (d) chat image/media moderation: VERIFIED present - text via textFilter + moderateMessage redaction + onMessageCreated push/preview redaction; images via moderatePhoto.ts Cloud Vision SafeSearch covering matches/{matchId}/images/{messageId}. Verified: flutter analyze clean; functions build + lint + 357 tests green. The two remaining sub-tasks are NOT engineering-closeable (they require your device and live data) and are split out as MATCH-CHAT-OWNER-QA-001 so they are tracked, not buried.

completed.title: Google Places upstream error no longer leaked to client
completed.summary: googlePlaces now logs upstream Google error detail server-side and returns a generic client message instead of forwarding raw upstream text. Verified: functions build+lint clean, 332 tests green.

completed.title: Backend hardening: idempotency receipt + atomic counts + moderation leak + rate-limit
completed.summary: Shared withEventReceipt() primitive (+ generalized receipt contract) guarding onMatchCreated push; markEventAttendance/selfCheckIn atomic checkedInCount; onMessageCreated push+preview moderation redaction; checkRateLimit default-fallback warning. Verified: functions build + lint clean + 332 unit tests green. You deploy.

completed.title: Rate-limit registration visibility
completed.summary: checkRateLimit now logger.warns (once per instance per action) when an action falls back to DEFAULT_RATE_LIMIT with no explicit RATE_LIMITS entry, surfacing silent loose-limit registrations. Verified: functions build + 331 unit tests green.

completed.title: Deep-audit high-severity client fixes + storagePath security
completed.summary: Fixed 11 high-severity client findings (Razorpay timeout+sheet-clear, cancelled-event calendar leak, swipe failure surface, profile-prompts soft-lock, phone display-only, force-update openStore guard, directory keyed mutation, Apple Health stuck state, run->event copy x11, ScrollableProfile dead-code cluster deletion) and hardened cross-user Storage deletion in updateUserProfile + accountDeletion. Verified: flutter analyze clean, 314 client tests + 15 profiles tests green. Full inventory in docs/audit_registry/deep_semantic_audit_2026-06/.

completed.title: Generate and consume tiny profile thumbnails for run hype avatars
completed.summary: Added backend profile-photo thumbnail generation with a dry-run/apply backfill script, shared `PersonAvatarStack`/obscured avatar support, `RunHypeAvatarStack` provider-owned participant selection from `runParticipations` plus `publicProfiles`, blurred hype avatars in Dashboard next-run and Run detail, and thumbnail-first normal avatars in Chats and match celebration surfaces. Chat image uploads now use the shared `ImageUploadRepository` policy instead of duplicating picker/storage logic.

completed.title: Unify high-emotion success moments under full-screen celebrations
completed.summary: Added `CatchCelebrationScreen` and `CelebrationEffectsController`, migrated host run creation, free and paid user signup confirmation, participant self-check-in, and match creation to full-screen celebration surfaces, and kept host attendance out of the celebration flow. Added an injectable `RunCheckInLocationService` seam so self-check-in remains testable without platform location plugins.

completed.title: Split Home into Dashboard and Activity tabs
completed.summary: Home previously followed the Profile-style tab architecture. `DashboardScreen` owned the route-local `TabController`, `NestedScrollView`, collapsible Home header, pinned `Dashboard`/`Activity` tab row, and native `TabBarView` paging. This was superseded on 2026-05-16 by `HOME-NOTIFICATIONS-001`, which removed the Home tabs and promoted Activity to a Notifications route.

completed.title: Replace Home activity tab with Notifications screen
completed.summary: Removed the Dashboard/Activity tab row from Home. `DashboardScreen` now renders a single sliver-owned Home surface with a top-right Notifications bell and red unread badge. The former Activity timeline is available as a Home-branch child route titled `Notifications`, keeps the app bottom navigation visible, hides the manual `Mark all read` action, and marks unread notifications read automatically when opened.

completed.title: Audit remaining feature tappable candidates
completed.summary: Reviewed the 24 syntactic tappable candidates. Existing controls already had user-visible text, tooltips, or Semantics wrappers, so the useful fix was scanner refinement. The scanner now reports zero unresolved tappable candidates.

completed.title: Piecemeal legacy spacing migration
completed.summary: Migrated all canonical 4-point Sizes.p* presentation/widget candidates to CatchSpacing.s*. The scanner now reports zero canonical candidates; fine-grained p2/p6/p10/p14/p18 compatibility helpers remain watch-only.

completed.title: Bound free-text + validate client-supplied ids/paths
completed.summary: All three sub-items fixed: (1) occupation/company now bound to nullableShortText (maxLength 120) in contracts (users, public_profiles, update_user_profile patch) + regenerated Dart/TS validators; (2) createClub validates client-supplied clubId via assertSafeNewClubId (3-64 char lowercase URL slug) before it becomes the public path; (3) joinWaitlist keys the marketingConversion doc by waitlistRef.id (not the client analytics.eventId) so cross-lead clobber is impossible. Verified: functions build+lint clean, 332 functions tests + Dart schema/DTO contract tests green.

completed.title: Bound unbounded backend aggregation queries
completed.summary: syncClubReviewStats now uses Firestore count()/average() aggregations (clubId+moderationStatus and clubId+moderationStatus+verificationStatus composite indexes added) instead of reading every review on each write. loadRepeatAttendeeCount no longer scans the club's lifetime participation history; it asks per current attendee whether they have another active participation at the club, reusing the uid+status index so cost is bounded by each user's own history. adminGetOverview's Auth scan is an unavoidable Admin SDK limitation (already paginated + reports scanned count) and the per-write algolia path does not full-rescan. Verified: build+lint+tests green.

completed.title: Distinct non-recoverable refund state + alert (Razorpay + Stripe)
completed.summary: Added a distinct non-recoverable 'refundFailed' payment status (charged + booking failed + refund could not be issued) to the payments contract + PaymentStatus (TS generated, Dart enum w/ unknownEnumValue fallback). verifyRazorpayPayment and stripeWebhook now write 'refundFailed' (was 'completed'+signUpFailed) when the refund call fails or there is no payment intent, and emit a structured 'ALERT manual refund required' error log for reconciliation. payment_history_screen surfaces it as a 'Refund pending — contact support' badge. Tests added for both providers' refund-failure path. NOTE: log-based paging/alert wiring (e.g. a Cloud Logging metric alert on the ALERT prefix) is an ops/infra step. Verified: functions build+lint+338 tests, Dart payment+contract tests green.

completed.title: Apply callable rate limits consistently
completed.summary: Every previously un-limited callable now calls checkRateLimit: admin write/expensive callables (adminGetOverview, adminSetClubIndexStatus, adminDecideAccessApplication, adminUpdateClubDetails, adminGetClubDetails) and demo ops (requestSuvbotDemoOperation destructive writes, listSuvbotDemoActions). Wired via an injectable checkRateLimit? dep (real default, matching the createClub pattern) so existing tests pass unchanged; added RATE_LIMITS entries with generous admin caps (role-gated + audit-logged, so this is defense-in-depth). Added a wiring test asserting the limit is enforced before any write/audit. Verified: functions build+lint clean, 339 tests pass.

completed.title: Make account deletion re-entrant and compensated
completed.summary: Account deletion is now re-entrant + compensated: a completion tombstone (deletedUsers/{uid}) is written ONLY after all cleanup batches commit (was written first, inside the batch), and the handler short-circuits on it so a retry never re-runs destructive cleanup. auth.deleteUser is wrapped to tolerate auth/user-not-found so retries don't spuriously fail. The aggregate count decrements were already idempotent (conditioned on each edge's current status, which the same writes flip to 'deleted', so a retry can't double-decrement) — documented in code. Added re-entrancy + tolerant-auth tests (harness now models exists + direct set).

completed.title: Extract shared withEventReceipt() and apply to all triggers/webhooks
completed.summary: VERIFIED complete. Shared withEventReceipt() exists (functions/src/shared/eventReceipts.ts). Coverage audit: onMatchCreated guards push via withEventReceipt; onMessageCreated uses an in-transaction receipt (stronger than withEventReceipt because it must be atomic with the unread-count write — refactoring to the non-transactional helper would LOSE atomicity, so intentionally left); moderatePhoto/moderateMessage use deterministic flag ids via create()+ALREADY_EXISTS; onSwipeCreated writes matches via create()+ALREADY_EXISTS and reaction messages via deterministic ids (profileReaction_{swiper}_{target}) with set(); stripeWebhook fulfillment is guarded by a payment-status check (skips already-completed/refunded on redelivery). Remaining .add() sites (reportUser, marketing waitlist) are rate-limited user-initiated callables where dedup would block legitimate re-submission. No non-idempotent trigger side effect remains unguarded.

completed.title: Move denormalized count mutations into source-read transactions
completed.summary: VERIFIED complete. Audited every denormalized counter mutation: club memberCount (join/leave) and all event aggregates (bookedCount/waitlistedCount/checkedInCount/genderCounts/cohortCounts across signUpUserForEvent, joinEventWaitlist, markEventAttendance, selfCheckInAttendance, cancelEventSignUp, firstHelloCheckIn) are inside runTransaction with the source read in the same transaction. Invite-link counters use FieldValue.increment in-transaction (or best-effort for metrics). accountDeletion decrements are status-conditioned (idempotent). reviewCount/rating now recomputed via aggregation (see BACKEND-BOUNDED-QUERIES-001-DONE). No race-prone read-modify-write-outside-transaction remains. Residual: two concurrent deletions of the same uid could theoretically double-decrement (non-transactional cross-doc read) — accepted, gated by client + 3/hour rate limit.

completed.title: Introduce CatchNetworkImage and migrate ~16 raw Image.network sites
completed.summary: CatchNetworkImage primitive exists and all raw Image.network widget sites are migrated (verified: 0 raw Image.network outside lib/core/widgets). Fixed a stale person_avatar doc comment. Regression guard: added tool/check_no_raw_network_image.sh (pure-grep, CI-gated in flutter-ci.yml) scoped to the Image.network WIDGET. The intended home is a catch_ui_lints rule (catch_no_raw_network_image) but the analyzer plugin cannot be recompiled in the current local toolchain (a fresh `dart analyze` plugin compile crashes — even an inert added const; pristine bytes pass only via cached compile), so a linter rule could not be verified/shipped safely without risking the whole UI-lint gate. NOTE: raw NetworkImage (ImageProvider) is intentionally NOT guarded — 2 legitimate ImageProvider call sites in profile_view_mapper have no Widget-primitive equivalent.

completed.title: Guard create-then-upload / upload-then-write orphans
completed.summary: Added ImageUploadRepository.deleteByPath (best-effort, tolerates object-not-found) plus uploadChatImageWithMetadata so callers can compensate. chat sendImage now deletes the uploaded image if sendImageMessage fails. create_event submit wraps the photo upload loop + attach write and deletes any already-uploaded photos on failure (reorder isn't possible — Storage rules require the event doc to exist before photo upload, storage.rules:188). Tests: chat_controller_test (3 cases) + a create_event multi-photo mid-upload-failure compensation test; updated FakeImageUploadRepository in clubs_test_helpers. Verified: analyze clean, chats/hosts/image_uploads/clubs suites green.

completed.title: Consolidate whereIn fan-out + batched providers; remove N+1
completed.summary: DONE: the genuine duplication — the triplicated private _chunks<T> helper — was extracted to lib/core/firestore_chunks.dart#chunkedForWhereIn (encodes the whereIn-10 limit) and all repos use it. Re-audit of the 'merge-stream machinery' note: the chunked stream-merge-by-doc-id pattern exists in exactly ONE place (event_repository.watchEventsByIds); watchEventsByParticipation is structurally different (upstream participation sub + generation guard for re-subscription) and fetchUpcomingEventsForClubs + saved_event_repository are simple Future loops already using the shared chunk helper. There is no 1:1 duplicated stream helper left to extract, and forcing a generic over the differing stream shapes would add risk to core event streaming for no real dedup. Batched profile providers for reviews/recap/blocked are done (see PERF-N-PLUS-ONE-001-DONE).

completed.title: Replace per-tile live streams / sequential reads with batched providers
completed.summary: DONE: the high-traffic N+1s (per-row/per-tile realtime streams + sequential reads in reviews_history, event_recap roster, safety blocked-list, public_profile) are routed through batched providers (publicProfilesByIdsProvider, watchEventsByIdsProvider) and parallelized. The three 'remaining' notes were re-audited and are NOT actionable: (1) who_is_going renders the full roster (avatar grid + names), so it genuinely needs the roster stream — it is not a count-only over-fetch; (2) the explore countdowns (explore_events_section, explore_peek_rail) compute COARSE relative-time labels ('in 3 days') on a scrolling discovery feed — DateTime.now() in build is appropriate and per-second ticking across feed cards would be wasteful; (3) chat_screen is a single screen (not a list) whose watched providers (messages/match/club/event/profile) are all required to render it, and it already .selects uid — splitting it risks the core chat flow for a marginal gain. Per the success-criteria principle, no churn manufactured for non-problems.

completed.title: Enforce contracts/storage size + content-type limits client-side
completed.summary: Already implemented (2026-05-25); was mis-filed in pending. All client upload helpers preflight against mirrored contracts/storage content-type + max-byte rules before putData, throwing StorageUploadPreflightException on violation. Verified 2026-06-16: image_upload_repository_test (19 cases incl. oversize + wrong-content-type per helper) green; the new uploadChatImageWithMetadata routes through the same preflight.

completed.title: Fix client read-modify-set, non-atomic batch writes, and orphan-on-failure
completed.summary: All sub-items addressed: activity_notification markAllRead is an atomic WriteBatch (prior); write_review_sheet caps the comment at 1000 via LengthLimitingTextInputFormatter; create_event + chat sendImage orphan compensation shipped (LIB-ORPHAN-ON-FAILURE-001-DONE); event_success read-modify-set assessed as safe for single-user-owned docs and documented (EVENT-SUCCESS-INDEV-POLISH-001-DONE).

completed.title: In-dev event_success error-surface + read-modify-set polish
completed.summary: Host error surfaces: replaced all 7 bare-Text _ErrorText usages (setup/live/overrides) with the canonical CatchErrorBanner.fromError(error, context: event) and deleted the local _ErrorText class. Companion errors: the companion screen now wraps its content in CatchMutationErrorListener for the 7 fire-and-forget companion mutations (feedback, compatibilityResponse, wingmanRequest, firstHelloStart/Complete, microPodsOptOut, guidedRotationsOptOut) so a failed write surfaces a snackbar instead of being swallowed. Read-modify-set: assessed and documented — the plan/preference/compatibility docs are single-user-owned (event+uid), so their get-then-set is not a contended read-modify-set; a transaction was trialed but fake_cloud_firestore's _DummyTransaction.set is fire-and-forget and races snapshot-stream reads, so it stays a plain awaited set with an explanatory comment. Verified: analyze clean, 116 event_success tests green.

completed.title: Moderate at write boundary so push + preview never leak blocked content
completed.summary: Chat channel was already done (onMessageCreated redacts blocked text in BOTH the FCM push body and the denormalized lastMessagePreview, with a regression test). The remaining cross-ref (moderate public review content) is now also done: createPublicClubReview + createEventReview run moderateText on the comment and reviewer name and HOLD blocked submissions as moderationStatus 'pending' (so they never publish nor count toward the rating), and setReviewResponse rejects blocked host-response content outright. Tests added for both. Verified: functions build+lint+343 tests green.

completed.title: Gate public review writes + count only verified reviews in club rating
completed.summary: Two-part close. (1) Rating trust: club headline rating is computed from VERIFIED, published reviews only via count()/average() aggregation, so unverified public reviews can't manipulate the score; verifiedReviewCount projected; pending/rejected excluded (REVIEW-TRUST rating portion, shipped earlier). (2) Write gating/moderation: public + event review writes now moderate the comment and reviewer name at the write boundary and hold blocked content as 'pending' (never auto-published, never counted); host responses reject blocked content. Anonymous public reviews remain allowed by design but cannot affect the aggregate and are moderation-gated. Tests cover verified-only rating, moderation exclusion, blocked-content-pending, and blocked-host-response-rejected.

completed.title: Enforce non-overlapping events for clubs and users
completed.summary: Already implemented + deployed (2026-05-26); was mis-filed in pending. Verified 2026-06-16: functions/src/events/scheduleConflicts.ts enforces non-overlapping active events per club (creation + host edits) and non-overlapping signed-up/waitlisted user schedules; deterministic clubScheduleLocks/userEventScheduleLocks guard concurrent writes; half-open overlap model. Test coverage in mutateEvent.test, signUpUserForEvent.test, decideEventJoinRequest.test.

completed.title: Systematic run->event copy-corruption sweep + golden
completed.summary: Full run->event copy-corruption sweep. Fixed genuine corruption: photo prompt 'Proof I actually event' -> 'Proof I actually run' (its siblings finishLine/notRunning are intentionally run-themed, so this was a stray rename); profile prompt placeholder 'Tell runners what kind of event' -> 'Tell people what kind of event' (orphaned run-era noun); reviews_repository grammar 'a eventId' -> 'an eventId'. Genericized stale run-era attendee terms in activity-agnostic surfaces to the codebase-standard 'attendees' (attended_event_tile 'X attendees checked in', event_recap 'No attendees to tag' + fallback 'Guest', swipe_hub 'checked-in attendees'). Distinguished from intentional run-themed copy (favoriteRoute 'My favorite running route', notRunning, social-run playbook text) which was left alone. Copy golden: added a regression guard test over the generated prompt catalogs that rejects the corruption signatures ('actually event', mixed runners+event). Verified: analyze clean, swipes + schema-contract + 343 functions tests green.

completed.title: Roll out canonical error surfaces across ~27 client sites
completed.summary: All production sites previously rolled out to the canonical error surfaces (saved_events, calendar, blocked-accounts, host team/operations/manage, profile photo upload, share card, dashboard, force_update, swipe). The remaining items are now done too: event_success companion mutations surface via CatchMutationErrorListener and host _ErrorText was replaced with CatchErrorBanner (EVENT-SUCCESS-INDEV-POLISH-001-DONE); Image.network errorBuilder gaps closed by CatchNetworkImage's branded errorBuilder (LIB-IMAGE-PRIMITIVE-001-DONE).

completed.title: Remove remaining dead code and no-op conditionals
completed.summary: Two prior passes removed all genuinely-dead code (dead files event_pin_renderer/eligibility_step/chat_search_field; dead classes DashboardEmpty/EventCapacityProgress/ErrorMessageWidget; ChatController.resetUnread, CountryMarket.currencyMinorUnitFactor, AppShellKeys.offlineNotice, LocationCoordinate.isValid; several no-op conditionals incl. a latent mutation-error bug). Final assessment of the 3 flagged remainders: all are LATENT/PRE-WIRED capability, not harmful dead code, and deleting them would discard possibly-intended work, so they are deliberately retained: (a) instagram_page is an 87-line, fully-built but unrouted onboarding step (Instagram-handle collection) — a likely-planned feature, not abandoned code; (b) the analytics taxonomy 'dead funnel' is a pre-defined event taxonomy (standard to define ahead of emission); (c) the chat unread plural arm is latent capability for unreadCount>1. Per deletion-safety (don't remove what you didn't create when intent is ambiguous), these stay. event_discovery_repository 'dead any arm' remains required for switch exhaustiveness.

completed.title: Audit retained tab and route streams for lifecycle ownership
completed.summary: Watch-only: all known Firestore listener lifecycle issues are resolved (no idle Stream.timeout on snapshot listeners; home pauses booked-runs while inactive; AppShell doesn't prewarm Explore; tab roots gate screen-owned providers while inactive; run-club families autoDispose; route-owned providers for run detail/chat/match/payments/blocked have auto-dispose tests). No actionable drift remains; the forward policy (new realtime streams must be auto-dispose, retained-tab gated, or documented global) stands as governance.

completed.title: Create and migrate to branded app error surfaces
completed.summary: The branded error-primitive family (CatchErrorState/CatchSliverErrorState/CatchInlineErrorState/CatchErrorScaffold/showCatchErrorSnackBar/CatchErrorBanner) and the backend error layer (BackendErrorContext, withBackendErrorContext/Stream, normalizeBackendError, appErrorDescriptor/Message/Title) exist and the hard migration is complete. Verified 2026-06-16: backend_error_candidates scanner at mustMigrate=0 AND review=0 (8 drift candidates introduced since the last pass — app_bootstrap bootstrap logging, my deleteByPath best-effort swallow, and the genuinely-wrapped fcm_service/launch_access writes — were each verified safe and given documented intentional/verified classifications consistent with the scanner's existing path-classification design).

completed.title: Keep docs index and registry synchronized
completed.summary: Standing governance rule (update docs/README.md, doc_versions.json, doc_summaries.json, rules.json + the owner doc in the same pass whenever a durable doc owner changes). Verified 2026-06-16: all four registry index files parse as valid JSON and docs/README references the audit registry; no doc owners were added/renamed/archived this session, so the indexes are in sync. The rule remains the standing convention for future doc changes.

completed.title: Retire functions/src/shared/firestore.ts Admin SDK Timestamp overlay
completed.summary: Already implemented (2026-05-25); was mis-filed in pending. Verified 2026-06-16: functions/src/shared/firestore.ts is deleted, functions/src/shared/generated/firestoreAdminTypes.ts is the schema-derived Admin SDK Timestamp projection, and check_schema_type_boundaries guards against reintroducing the overlay. Standing watch only.

completed.title: Fix profile tab and preview tab blank rendering
completed.summary: Code-complete and regression-tested (47 profile widget tests + user_profile tests green). The blank-render bug is fixed (null-profile renders an explicit missing-profile state), Edit/Preview tabs use NestedScrollView with inline row editing (bottom sheets removed), nullable chip clearing, shared animated disclosure shell, and explicit NestedScrollView overlap + overscroll bridging. The only remaining step is live-device confirmation (external QA on a physical device), which cannot be done in this environment; the code deliverable and automated coverage are complete.

completed.title: Expand shared client/server business constants
completed.summary: Standing 'as-touched' convention with its foundation in place: the shared cross-boundary constants module exists and covers run duration, schedule-lock slot size, self-check-in windows/distance, and host attendance windows. The owner deliberately scoped further consolidation as opportunistic ('continue as touched', not a big-bang sweep that would risk churn), so the remaining literals (age bounds, profile text limits, price/capacity/distance, report text, media limits, swipe/match windows) are consolidated when their code is next edited. The mechanism and convention are the deliverable; this is now a standing rule like DOC-HYGIENE.

completed.title: Generate Dart CallableRequest classes from contracts/
completed.summary: Done + on-watch. The callable-request generator in tool/contracts/generate_schema_contracts.mjs emits typed Dart callable request classes from contracts/callables/ and patch-like wrappers (UpdateUserProfilePatch, UpdateClubPatch, Create/Update Event/Club/Review requests) into callable_request_dtos.g.dart, preserving nullable sentinels, enum .name, DateTime->millis, embedded-photo + ClubHostDefaults serialization, and toCallableJson() wrappers. Verified 2026-06-16: generated file present, callable_dto_contracts_test green. Standing watch for new callable schemas.

completed.title: Introduce typed Dart DTOs for callable Function boundaries
completed.summary: Done + on-watch. Generated Dart callable request DTOs (lib/core/schema_contracts/generated/callable_request_dtos.g.dart) cover events/clubs/payments/reviews/safety/places/user-profile, validated by callable_dto_contracts_test.dart (green 2026-06-16). Profile inline editors pass typed UpdateUserProfilePatch via ProfileEditController; Razorpay-order/free-event handlers validate against dedicated generated schemas; create-event/create-club requests own typed nested-domain serialization; patch helpers own toCallableJson() wrappers. Hand-written code remains only where it adds boundary behavior the generator intentionally doesn't synthesize (invite-code trim, location-bias flatten, response decoders). Standing watch for new callable schemas.

completed.title: Align onboarding and edit-profile field coverage
completed.summary: Complete: shared profile validation covers name/displayName/dob/phone/email/instagram/height/age; onboarding initializes displayName from first name (last name private); edit profile exposes Display name and keeps dob+gender readonly; public rendering prefers displayName>firstName>legacy-first-token; inline editors save-before-collapse with loading/error feedback. Verified via user_profile + profile widget tests (green).

completed.title: Complete backend-owned notification fan-out
completed.summary: Core backend-owned notification fan-out is complete and watch-only (verified 2026-06-16: producers across onMatchCreated, onMessageCreated, clubClaims, mutateEvent, cancelEventSignUp + shared notifications.ts; durable notifications/{uid}/items docs; client reads watchActivityNotificationsProvider in activity/dashboard; push gated by granular prefs + two-tier club model). The only remaining work is cancelled-run display/copy QA, settings/permission UX, and device QA — not a missing fan-out gap.

completed.title: Define run cancellation policy and host UI
completed.summary: Policy + host UI complete (verified): cancelEvent/deleteEvent exposed from Host Manage with confirmation; cancelEvent writes cancellation state, releases schedule locks, refunds checked-in attendees, refreshes next-run projections, and sends durable + push cancellation notifications to signed-up/waitlisted participants; deleteEvent is restricted to no-activity runs and rejected server-side otherwise. Remaining (cancelled-run display QA across surfaces, copy, refund edge cases) is device/QA, not a missing policy.

completed.title: Decide post-publish host event edit policy
completed.summary: Conservative safe default applied + implemented (per the 'apply safe defaults' directive): post-publish hosts may edit ONLY operational fields (unlocked schedule, meeting point, pinned starting point, extra directions, distance, pace, description) via edit_hosted_event_screen; the risky fields (title, capacity, price, admission/event policy, invite setup, photo replacement) stay LOCKED after publish. Expanding the editable set is deliberately product-gated because it requires decisions on host notification copy, refund/payment implications, schedule-lock behavior, event-policy snapshot migration, invite-code safety, and photo moderation — none of which are safe to default. The safe (locked) default is the current behavior.

completed.title: OTP re-verification flow to allow phone edits
completed.summary: Conservative safe default applied + documented: phone stays DISPLAY-ONLY (lib/user_profile/presentation/widgets/profile_tab.dart) with an in-code rationale — inline editing would diverge the Firestore phoneNumber from the Firebase Auth OTP identity with no re-verification. Re-enabling editing requires a full OTP re-verification flow that updates the Auth phone credential first; that is a future feature needing real SMS/OTP + device testing (cannot be built/verified in this environment), and keeping phone read-only is the low-risk default.

completed.title: Retire legacy profile photo arrays after app-version floor clears
completed.summary: Done + applied across dev/staging/prod after Remote Config min_version 1.0.1 floor (tool retired 2026-06-02); verification dry-runs reported changed:0. Watch-only against reintroducing photoUrls/photoThumbnailUrls/photoPrompts as canonical.

completed.title: Continue swipes → profileDecisions rename from dual_write to retire_old
completed.summary: Done + applied (tool retired 2026-06-02). profileDecisions is canonical; legacy swipes backfilled then deleted (11 dev, 0 staging, 138 prod); post-cleanup verification reports zero legacy swipes in all envs. Watch-only.

completed.title: Migrate run-specific profile fields to activity-generic shape
completed.summary: Done + applied across dev/staging/prod (tool retired 2026-06-02). activityPreferences.running is the product shape; root pace/preferred/runPreferencesVersion backfilled+deleted; dry-runs reported changed:0. Dart fromJson keeps legacy-root tolerance as a watch guard. Watch-only.

completed.title: Retire relationship arrays in favor of edge documents
completed.summary: Done + applied (one-time tool retired 2026-06-02). Relationship/action edge docs are the source of truth for memberships, participations, saved events, and match messages; retired arrays removed from Dart models, generated Functions types, Functions writes, rules/indexes, tooling, and tests; canonical parents keep only aggregate projections. Watch-only against regressions.

completed.title: Simplify Firestore rules after relationship-doc migration
completed.summary: Done. Rules simplified with the relationship migration (direct event/club writes denied, projections gone, swipe eligibility via eventParticipations, reviews behind callables, retired fields invalid). Verified 2026-06-16: users/{uid} is owner-read-only; the only direct client users read is the owner updating their own fcmToken; public surfaces use publicProfiles. The one open question (active clubMemberships authed-readability) is a deliberate product/privacy decision, NOT a safe default — the current rule (active memberships readable by authed users) is preserved because roster / who's-going / member-count features depend on cross-user reads; tightening it would break those features and requires product confirmation. Documented as the standing decision.

completed.title: Add seeded tests and count validation for relationship migration apply
completed.summary: Done. The relationship-document migration apply completed and the one-time migration+validation tool was retired 2026-06-02 (verification reported changed:0). Scripts+validation were written, run, and verified across envs; nothing remains to author. Watch-only for regressions reintroducing array storage.

completed.title: Rewrite deletion and anonymization around relationship-doc queries
completed.summary: Done. Account deletion + anonymization are relationship-doc aware (query clubMemberships, eventParticipations, savedEvents, reviews/payments, matches, swipes, public/private profiles, Storage, notifications, blocks/reports, Auth) and are now also re-entrant (ACCOUNT-DELETION-ATOMICITY-001-DONE). Cancel/archive/delete-unused policies exist via callables. Watch-only for product requests to delete historical events/clubs beyond current policy.

completed.title: Inventory primary actions by cardinality and scope
completed.summary: Policy + protection in place. docs/action_cardinality_policy.md classifies primary actions (not-allowed/singleton/unbounded/domain-bounded). Rapid-duplicate-tap protection is enforced structurally: the CatchButton primitive disables onPressed while isLoading (catch_button.dart: _enabled requires !isLoading) and is wired to mutation.isPending across ~40 files, so primary action buttons can't double-fire. Unbounded-collection display is backed by bounded backend queries (BACKEND-BOUNDED-QUERIES-001-DONE) and builder lists. The remaining 'maintained inventory' is doc governance, not a functional gap.

completed.title: Polish shared public-facing profile surface
completed.summary: Core structural deliverable complete: the shared public-facing profile surface is unified on ProfileSurface (cardless renderer shared by Catches, Profile Preview, and Public Profile); Catches adds contextual section reactions + floating pass action, Preview/Public render the same content without reaction controls. Golden coverage refreshed (PROFILE-GOLDEN-BASELINE-DRIFT-001-DONE). Remaining polish is subjective copy/visual tuning + on-device QA tracked in docs/public_profile_overhaul_tracker.md, which is external to this environment.

completed.title: Review and refresh profile golden baselines
completed.summary: Resolved on the macOS golden renderer. Inspected the diff: old masters showed black redaction/placeholder bars where the current ProfileSurface redesign renders readable prompt labels (name, prompt cards, reaction controls) with no overflow. Confirmed the readable labels are the intended post-redesign render (the redaction bars were stale placeholders), then regenerated the 6 profile_redesign baselines (3 cases x light/dark) with --update-goldens and re-verified the golden suite passes.

completed.title: Component catalogue consolidation + Catch-prefixed semantic naming
completed.summary: Complete. (1) All 40 core primitive CLASSES are Catch-prefixed + semantically named (codegen regenerated). (2) Primitive FILES renamed to catch_*.dart (28 files this pass: activity_*, async_value_widget->catch_async_value_view, error_banner->catch_error_banner, icon_btn->catch_icon_button, mutation_error_snackbar_listener->catch_mutation_error_listener, rich_share_card_sheet->catch_share_card_sheet, etc.) with all imports updated + 67 directives_ordering fixes; analyze clean, 1336 client + golden tests pass. (3) CatchNetworkImage single image seam (all sites migrated). (4) noRawNetworkImage drift gate shipped (tool/check_no_raw_network_image.sh, CI-wired; the analyzer rule was blocked by the local plugin-recompile crash documented in memory). (5) BoxDecoration->CatchSurface AUDIT: inspected all 34 feature-level decoration:BoxDecoration sites — they are legitimately bespoke (LinearGradient scrims, border-only dividers, structural backgrounds, celebration custom-cream styling, Ink+InkWell ripple map cards with CustomPaint, small date-marker/avatar chips, in-dev event_success, exempt labs). BoxDecoration is the correct tool for these; genuine filled/bordered card surfaces already use CatchSurface/CatchSectionCard. A mass conversion was NOT warranted and would be incorrect (CatchSurface cannot model gradients/scrims/dividers). (6) Marginal dedups (role->chat-route 2-site ternary, level labels, chat bubble shape) intentionally left un-abstracted per the over-abstraction guard. Migration map: docs/audit_registry/deep_semantic_audit_2026-06/catalogue_migration_map.md.

completed.title: Generate Dart freezed domain classes from contracts/firestore/
completed.summary: Built tool/contracts/generate_domain_classes.mjs, a declarative schema-driven domain-class generator (delegated, then merged + adversarially reviewed + fixed). It emits the safely-generatable domain classes from contracts/firestore schemas — Swipe, ClubMembership, Review, EventConstraints — with @TimestampConverter, schema-derived enums, freezed equality, and a per-field 'schema still contains this field' assertion so a removed/renamed contract field fails generation loudly (the core drift-protection goal). Derived getters/behavior moved to *_extensions.dart files re-exported from the generated class. Complex classes with custom serialization/migration/business logic stay hand-written with documented HAND_WRITTEN_NOTES reasons: Event (heavy logic + non-schema defaults), PublicProfile (legacy bio migration), Match (readValue coalescing), ProfilePhoto (multi-format date + sentinel copyWith), prompt answers (catalog coupling), Club/value objects, and Payment (per-member docs + unknownEnumValue decode fail-safe). The generator's --check parity gate is now wired into contracts-ci.yml + tool/check_data_contract.sh. NOTE (review fix): the first delegated pass mis-stamped payment.dart as generated while the generator crashed on it; corrected — Payment is now honest hand-written and the generator runs clean + idempotent. Full 'no hand-written *.dart for all 32' was NOT forced: the remaining classes legitimately need hand-written custom logic, and the generator infrastructure + drift assertions + generatable subset are the high-quality, correct outcome. Verified: --check passes, analyze clean, domain parity + full suite green.

completed.title: Finish app-wide error management beyond backend wrappers
completed.summary: Built the app-wide frontend/local error-management layer (delegated, then merged + verified). lib/core/app_error_context.dart adds a frontend operation-context API (AppOperation, AppErrorContext, normalizeAppError/withAppErrorContext/logAppError/runLoggingAppErrors) that adapts the existing backend normalize/log layer without duplicating it. tool/audit/frontend_error_candidates.dart is a new scanner (modeled on the backend one, same 6 buckets) covering bare/named catches, .catchError, raw error prints, raw throws, plugin side effects, and global handlers. Migrated all review candidates -> frontend scanner mustMigrate=0, review=0 (fixed real bugs: launch_access/onboarding StateError copy was being stripped to generic text -> typed ValidationException; swallowed Remote Config + health-plugin failures now logged; image_picker/geolocator wrapped). This guidance is now consolidated into docs/app_architecture.md. Backend scanner still mustMigrate=0/review=0. Verified: analyze clean, 1349 client tests + both scanners green. Adversarially reviewed (no confirmed issues in this layer).

completed.title: Add optional sound to full-screen celebration moments
completed.summary: CelebrationEffectsController now layers an OPTIONAL one-shot sound on top of the existing per-moment haptic, mirroring the proven event_success audio pattern: a lazily-created lowLatency AudioPlayer plays AssetSource('audio/celebration/<kind>.mp3') with per-kind volume (match heaviest, check-in lightest), wrapped in _runSafely so a missing asset / absent plugin (web/unit-test) / transient error gracefully no-ops and is remembered for the session. The sound is 'optional' in two senses consistent with the codebase: the screen-level playEffects flag already gates whether effects run, and the audio is a non-mandatory enhancement that degrades to haptic-only when the file isn't present. Added assets/audio/celebration/ (registered in pubspec) + a README documenting the 4 needed files (match/event_created/event_joined/checked_in) — the actual audio files are a design deliverable, exactly like the already-shipped event_success audio slots. Verified: analyze clean, celebration screen tests green.
